🎙 Section 1 · Episode 1 of 2 — Why It Matters
ECM Step-Up Documentation — Why It Matters
Anna and Brian set the stage — what is a Step-Up, why accurate documentation is non-negotiable, and what's at stake.

What Is a CalAIM ECM Step-Up?

A Step-Up encounter is initiated when a Care Manager reaches out to a patient who qualifies for Enhanced Care Management. It is the first structured touchpoint that opens the ECM episode, establishes the care team, completes SDoH screening, and creates the billing record that supports the program's sustainability.

Why Documentation Quality Is Compliance

CalAIM ECM encounters are subject to audit by DHCS and SFHP. Every field — encounter type, program, LOS, smartphrase, provider authorization — must be completed correctly. Errors can result in claim denial, audit findings, or program risk. This SOP is your protection.

Policy & Standards
DHCS: CalAIM Enhanced Care Management Policy Guide SFHP: Enhanced Care Management Provider Resources NCQA: Managed Care Organization Standards — Care Coordination
🎙 Section 1 · Episode 2 of 2 — Policy Context
CalAIM, SFHP & OCHIN — The Policy Ecosystem
Brian connects the dots between CalAIM policy, SFHP VBP requirements, OCHIN's Compass Rose tools, and NCQA care coordination standards that govern ECM documentation.
The ECM Documentation Ecosystem

Compass Rose

OCHIN Tier 2 population health tool providing care gap workqueues, ECM registry management, and attribution monitoring. Accessible via the Compass Rose button in the top-right corner of Epic.

SFHP VBP Requirements

San Francisco Health Plan value-based payment contracts require accurate ECM encounter data for quality reporting and shared savings calculations. Documentation errors directly impact VBP performance.

NCQA Standards

NCQA Managed Care Organization accreditation requires documented care coordination activities, SDoH screening, and care team assignment — all captured in this Step-Up workflow.

Resources
DHCS: CalAIM Enhanced Care Management Policy Guide OCHIN: Compass Rose Population Health Tools NCQA: Care Coordination & Quality Standards
🎙 Section 2 · Episode 1 of 3 — The Encounter
Steps 1–4: Opening the Encounter & Creating the Program
Brian walks through logging in, creating the 69 Patient Outreach encounter, documenting contacts and care team, and setting up the CalAIM ECM program.
1
Log Into Epic & Search for the Patient
  • Open Epic and log in with your MNHC credentials.
  • In the search bar, type 'encounter' and select Encounter under Patient Activity.
  • Navigate to the Chart section and search for your patient by name or MRN.
2
Create a 69 Patient Outreach Encounter
  • Select 69 Patient Outreach Encounter as the encounter type.
  • Add yourself as the encounter provider. Click Accept.
  • The encounter will open in the Initiation tab automatically.
3
Document Patient Contact in the Initiation Tab
  • In the Initiation tab, input details under Contacts: Contact Name, Relationship, Phone Number, and Outcome (required).
  • Record that you contacted the patient by phone or in person. Document as a successful contact and note the communication method.
  • Scroll down to Care Teams — add yourself as the Team Member.
  • Set Role: Lead Care Manager and Specialty. Select Yes to receive notifications. Click Accept.
📌 Always select the correct provider AND location when adding yourself to the Care Team. Do not select external organizations.
4
Navigate to the Care Mgmt Tab — Create Program
  • Click the Care Mgmt tab.
  • Under Program, click +New Program. If an old or incorrect program exists, disenroll it first.
  • Set: Program = CalAIM ECM (ECM cases) or Case Management (non-ECM). Use program-specific naming per your service line.
  • Enrollment Reason = Care Coordination  ·  Responsible Staff = Provider (yourself)  ·  Status = Outreach
🎙 Section 2 · Episode 2 of 3 — The Encounter
Steps 5–7: Outreach, Screenings & Scheduling
Brian covers tracking patient outreach, completing the AllSDoH screening with the OurPractice Advisory popup, and booking the follow-up appointment.
5
Navigate to the PT Outreach Section
  • Select the Pt Outreach section or scroll down to Track Pt Outreach.
  • Set Contacted Via: Telephone
  • Set Contacted About: Care Coordination
  • Establish and document a Next Contact date.
6
Complete the Screenings Section
  • Navigate to the Screenings section of the encounter.
  • Complete the screening geared toward your assigned discipline.
  • For SDoH / AllSDoH screening: select All SDH Domains. Complete the full AllSDoH screening — not a generic screening set.
  • A "OurPractice Advisory" pop-up will appear — click Add Problem for each identified SDoH issue. Click Accept.
  • Click Close when the screening is complete.
📌 Resource Connection staff must complete the full AllSDoH screening — not a generic or alternate screening set.
7
Schedule the Next Appointment
  • Navigate to the Appointment Desk and use Book It to schedule an Outreach appointment.
  • This appointment must match the documented Next Contact date from Step 5.
  • For patients with a high no-show history, use an Adhoc 69 encounter as an alternative follow-up method.
🎙 Section 3 · Episode 1 of 2 — Documentation
Steps 8–11: DX, SmartPhrases, LOS & Signing
Brian walks through adding diagnoses from the Problem List, activating the correct smartphrases, selecting CHG-15 as Level of Service, and signing the encounter correctly.
8
Add Diagnoses (DX)
  • Under Visit Diagnosis, add the primary DX: Encounter for counseling for care management of chronic conditions and complex health needs using nurse-based model (Dx Code: 1312356).
  • Also add all other relevant diagnoses from the Problem List.
  • Example: If the patient screened positive for Food Insecurity, add Food Insecurity as an additional DX.
9
Document Your Note — SmartPhrases
  • Complete the smartphrase .CALAIMSTEPUP — this is your primary note template.
  • The smartphrase auto-pulls components from earlier steps. Ensure all prior steps are complete before activating.
  • Based on your discipline, activate the corresponding Step-Up smartphrase: .STEPUPRC, .STEPUPSTRESS, .STEPUPDM, .STEPUPHTN, .STEPUPOBESITY, .STEPUPBH, .STEPUPPREVENTION, etc.
  • If outreach was unsuccessful, use .CALAIMUNSUCCESSFUL instead of .CALAIMSTEPUP.
⚠ Do NOT use alternative smartphrases. Only use the designated ECM smartphrases listed in the SmartPhrase Reference episode.
10
Add Level of Service (LOS)
  • Add CHG-15 / CHG-14 (non-clinical) or CHG-09 / CHG-11 (clinical) as the Level of Service.
  • Full LOS name: ECM Outreach Telephonic/Electronic Non-Clinical / Case Management
  • Review all steps and ensure all required fields are complete before proceeding.
⚠ Using an incorrect LOS may result in a billing compliance issue. CHG-15/14 or 09/11 is the required LOS for this Step-Up encounter.
11
Sign the Encounter
  • Review the full encounter for completeness before signing.
  • Provider authorization must be assigned to the patient's Primary Care Provider (PCP) — not the care manager.
  • Ensure documentation is timely, accurate, and HIPAA-compliant.
  • Always use Patient Outreach as the encounter type.
📌 Documentation must be completed and signed within the timeframe required by MNHC policy and CalAIM program guidelines.
🎙 Section 3 · Episode 2 of 2 — Documentation
SmartPhrase Quick Reference
Anna asks Brian to walk through every ECM smartphrase — when to use each one and which discipline it belongs to.
SmartPhraseWhen to Use
.CALAIMSTEPUPPrimary smartphrase — use when outreach was SUCCESSFUL (patient contacted). Pulls in contacts, care team, program, and screeners from earlier steps.
.CALAIMUNSUCCESSFULUse when unable to reach patient. Documents attempted outreach with reason and next contact plan.
.STEPUPRCResource Connection step-up. Activates after .CALAIMSTEPUP when patient is referred for social services, housing, or community resources.
.STEPUPSTRESSStress Management / Behavioral Health. Activates after .CALAIMSTEPUP for stress, anxiety, or mental health-related step-ups.
.STEPUPDMDisease Management step-up. Use for diabetes management step-ups.
.STEPUPHTNHypertension step-up. Use when hypertension is the primary driver.
.STEPUPOBESITYObesity / Weight Management step-up.
.STEPUPBHBehavioral Health integration step-up. For BH co-management not captured under stress.
.STEPUPPREVENTIONPreventive Care step-up. Use when preventive screenings or wellness interventions are the focus.
🎙 Section 4 · Episode 1 of 2 — Compliance
Documentation & Compliance Standards
Anna and Brian cover the non-negotiable compliance rules that apply to every ECM Step-Up encounter — and what happens when documentation falls short.

⚠ Non-Negotiable Rules

  • Always use 69 Patient Outreach as the encounter type
  • Only use designated ECM smartphrases — no substitutions
  • Provider authorization = patient's PCP, not the care manager
  • Complete AllSDoH screening (Resource Connection: full AllSDoH only)
  • LOS must be CHG-15/14 or CHG-09/11 — no other LOS permitted
  • Documentation must be timely and HIPAA-compliant

✓ Best Practices

  • Complete all steps before activating .CALAIMSTEPUP
  • Always select the correct MNHC location when adding yourself to Care Teams
  • Match the Book It appointment date to the documented Next Contact date
  • Add all SDoH diagnoses from the Problem List — not just the primary DX
  • Review all fields for completeness before signing
  • If patient not reached, use .CALAIMUNSUCCESSFUL immediately
🎙 Section 4 · Episode 2 of 2 — Compliance
Common Errors & How to Avoid Them
Brian walks through the most common documentation errors found in ECM Step-Up encounters and exactly how to prevent each one.
Error 1
Wrong encounter type selected
Fix: Always search for "encounter" and select 69 Patient Outreach Encounter. Do not use clinical encounter types for ECM outreach.
Error 2
Provider added with wrong location
Fix: When searching yourself in Care Teams, select your name paired with the correct MNHC site. Reject external organization matches.
Error 3
Generic screening used instead of AllSDoH
Fix: Resource Connection staff must always select "All SDH Domains" — not AHC Core, PRAPARE, or individual SDoH domains alone.
Error 4
Discipline smartphrase skipped
Fix: After .CALAIMSTEPUP, always activate the discipline-specific smartphrase (.STEPUPRC, .STEPUPHTN, etc.) before signing. The discipline screener must appear in the note.
Error 5
Care manager set as authorizing provider
Fix: The authorizing provider on the LOS and at signing must be the patient's PCP — never the care manager. Verify before clicking Sign Encounter.
Error 6
Appointment not booked or wrong date
Fix: Always use Book It to schedule the follow-up Outreach appointment immediately. The date must match the Next Contact date documented in Step 5.
